---
name: Spec Change Proposal (SCP)
about: Propose a breaking change, new feature, or governance change to the A2H protocol
title: "SCP: <short title>"
labels: ["scp", "needs-sponsor"]
---

<!-- Editorial fixes and non-breaking clarifications do NOT need an SCP — open a PR directly.
     See CONTRIBUTING.md for the editorial-vs-proposal split. -->

## Preamble
- **Author(s):**
- **Status:** Draft
- **Type:** <!-- Standards Track (normative) | Informational | Process -->
- **Created:** <!-- YYYY-MM-DD -->
- **Linked PR:** <!-- added once an implementation PR is opened -->

## Abstract
<!-- ~200 words: the change and the problem it addresses. -->

## Motivation
<!-- Why is the current spec inadequate? Concrete use cases. What stays broken or painful if we do nothing? -->

## Specification
<!-- Normative text (MUST / SHOULD / MAY), detailed enough for independent, interoperable implementations.
     Reference the affected schema files and spec sections. -->

## Rationale & alternatives
<!-- Why this design? What was considered and rejected? What is the cost of not doing it? -->

## Backward compatibility
<!-- Incompatibilities and migration path. State the version bump this triggers — MINOR (non-breaking
     addition) or MAJOR (breaking) — and whether a schema `$id` / version path changes. -->

## Security considerations
<!-- REQUIRED. New attack surfaces opened or closed; auth/authz, data exposure, replay/injection,
     trust-boundary impact. Per IETF RFC 3552, a substantive "none" is rarely credible. -->

## Conformance
<!-- Which conformance vectors must be added or changed? List each normative MUST/SHOULD and its check. -->

## Reference implementation
<!-- A prototype demonstrating feasibility: a branch in `reference/`, a PoC, or integration tests.
     A design doc with no code is not sufficient to reach Accepted. -->

## Unresolved questions
<!-- What must be resolved before this is accepted? What can defer to implementation? What is out of scope? -->

## Future possibilities
<!-- Optional: natural extensions this enables. Not a commitment. -->
